input.title: AEPD v. Tools for Humanity (Worldcoin) — precautionary cessation order under GDPR Art. 66(1) for unlawful iris-scan biometric processing in Spain
input.description: Spanish Data Protection Agency ordered Tools for Humanity Corporation to immediately cease the collection and processing of biometric (iris-scan) personal data carried out in Spain via its Worldcoin project, and to block data already collected. AEPD acted under the urgent-procedure mechanism of GDPR Art. 66(1) for a maximum 3-month period; Audiencia Nacional rejected Worldcoin's stay request on 11 March 2024; AEPD issued a Feb 2025 follow-up resolution requiring deletion of all iris data collected in Spain.

events.description: Orb operators captured iris scans of data subjects in Spain in exchange for Worldcoin tokens; AEPD complaint record cites insufficient information notices and inability to withdraw consent.
Iris-recognition engine generated and stored biometric IrisCode templates — special-category processing under GDPR Art. 9(1).
Reported collection of data from minors without effective age verification; consent-withdrawal mechanism unavailable in practice.
